extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won the uphill sprint after stage was shortened 70km due to weather, neutralizing the GC contenders on a single climb before attacking downhill finale
tactical_patterns: Emergency course shortening eliminates multiple climbs, favoring sprinting GC leaders over pure climbers in reshuffled tactical hierarchy
MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe positioned as key breakaway rider for Jumbo-Visma before course change, showing team's multi-scenario preparation
ROGLIČ attacked with 300m on downhill finish section, exploiting wet conditions where handling skill outweighs pure sprint power
Team Jumbo-Visma won positioning battle at base of remaining climb over Bora and Katusha, converting organizational strength into placement advantage

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: won second sprint finish in four days, demonstrating unexpected repeatability in uphill power finishes beyond TT specialty
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: third place suggests strong Tour preparation despite poor season start, positioning well for reduced-climbing scenario
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: present in finale but unable to convert climbing stage into win when multiple mountains removed
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: mentioned as contender but faded in finale, suggesting vulnerability when stage profile becomes less selective
rider_signals.GROßSCHARTNER Felix: neutral: animated in closing kilometers but positioning didn't convert to result despite aggressive intent
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: neutral: deployed in early break before course change, caught when stage became sprint-weighted rather than attrition-based
